questions.question: A surgeon is performing a parotidectomy and needs to identify the most superficial structure within the gland to avoid injuring it. Which of the following is the most superficial?
A patient is diagnosed with a parotid duct stone (sialolithiasis). The physician plans to examine the opening of the duct in the oral cavity. Where should the physician look?
options: Floor of the mouth near the tongue

questions.question: During a deep face laceration repair, the doctor notes a severed duct running across the masseter muscle. To locate the duct using surface anatomy, a line is drawn between the tragus and the midpoint of the upper lip. Which part of the line represents the duct?
A 55-year-old male patient underwent surgery for the removal of the parotid gland. Which of the following sites does its duct open?
A 72-year-old patient has lost taste sensation on the anterior two-third of the tongue. This is probably due to injury to branches of which of the following nerves?

A 42-year-old patient has lost general sensation on the anterior two-thirds of the tongue following radical resection of a primary tongue tumor. This is probably due to injury to branches of which of the following nerves?
